command.spark.physics.step.help:
=== Spark Physics Step Commands ===
Physics time speed control commands for managing performance under load

/spark physics step get - Get current physics step parameters
  Shows current dynamic step count, min/max step limits

/spark physics step set <min> <max> - Set physics step range
  min: Minimum step count (≥1), max: Maximum step count (≥min)
  Example: /spark physics step set 2 10

/spark physics step min <value> - Set minimum physics step
  value: Minimum step count (≥1)
  Example: /spark physics step min 3

/spark physics step max <value> - Set maximum physics step
  value: Maximum step count (≥current min value)
  Example: /spark physics step max 15

/spark physics step reset - Reset physics step to default values
  Restores to default min 1 step, max 5 steps

/spark physics step help - Show this help message

Explanation:
• Physics step controls the physics time multiplier (simulations per tick)
• System automatically adjusts within min-max range based on performance
• Higher step values = faster physics time, but more performance cost
• Lower step values = slower physics time, but better performance
